channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How Ticketmaster Sells Seats Without Double-Selling Them
item.description: A big show goes on sale. In the first minute, hundreds of thousands of people all want the same few good seats. Two people cannot buy seat 12A. The system has to pick a winner, tell the loser...

item.title: Proximity Search: How Yelp Finds Places Near You
item.description: Open Yelp, ask for coffee shops within a mile, and results come back instantly. Under that simple request is a genuinely hard database question: how do you efficiently find all points near a given...

item.title: Coming up for Air
item.description: After the absolute gauntlet of the past few weeks, this week finally offered a little bit of breathing room. I'm not going to lie, I needed it. But "lighter" in the open-source world doesn't mean...

item.title: I Rebuilt the Foundation of My Text Editor
item.description: After working on cdin for a while, I started noticing something. The biggest problems were not the features I was missing. They were the things underneath. When I first started cdin, the goal was...

item.title: Dev Opportunity Radar #7: $1,000 Solo Grants, Free Claude Max for Open Source Contributors, and an MLH Hackathon
item.description: TL;DR Welcome back to Dev Opportunity Radar. This is a weekly series where I share opportunities, resources, communities, and interesting finds that I come across, with the goal of helping people...

item.title: Burn After Reading
item.description: My manager ruined a perfectly good beer at an open air brewery in Richmond by telling me the truth about my career. The advice aged better than the beer. Here's the T-shaped engineer pitch nobody...

item.title: RDRIR: Real Danger Ramdom In Ruby
item.description: # RDRIR - Real Danger Random In Ruby # # ADVERTENCIA: Este generador de números "aleatorios" funciona # leyendo memoria cruda del heap de MRI a través de la dirección # de un objeto Random recién...

item.title: How Google Drive Syncs Files With Chunking and Deduplication
item.description: You edit one paragraph in a 50 MB document and it syncs in a second. You upload a file your coworker already uploaded and it finishes instantly. Neither of these is magic. Both fall out of two...

item.title: It Works on My Machine: A Docker War Story About exec format error
item.description: "It works on my machine" is the oldest joke in software, and containers were supposed to kill it. Same image everywhere, same behavior everywhere — that's the whole pitch. So there's a special...

item.title: Designing a Distributed Job Scheduler That Runs Jobs Exactly Once
item.description: You want to run a job every night at 2am. Easy, cron does that. Now you want to run a million jobs, each on its own schedule, across a fleet of machines where any node can die mid-execution, and...

item.title: I spent a week using the Trump phone — it sucks
item.description: The Trump phone was never a serious phone. Not when it was announced last June, in dodgy renders and with an incoherent spec sheet. Nor when Trump Mobile admitted - just two weeks later - that it...

item.title: Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
item.description: Details have emerged about three now-patched security flaws in the OpenClaw personal artificial intelligence (AI) assistant that, if successfully exploited, could enable credential theft,...

item.title: I’m filling in at The Verge for 6 weeks. Ask me anything!
item.description: Hello my friends! My protocol pals! My computational compadres! I'm David, a technology reporter and cohost of the Waveform Podcast. I'll be hosting a subscriber-only "AMA" today at 11AM PT / 2PM...

item.title: Empathy for the optimizers
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
item.description: Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter...

item.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
item.description: A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is...

item.title: New MODBEACON RAT Uses gRPC Streaming for Encrypted C2 Traffic
item.description: The China-linked cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been attributed to a new Rust-based remote access trojan (RAR) called MODBEACON. Chinese cybersecurity company QiAnXin said that while the...

item.title: Xreal’s new AR glasses are way cheaper and almost just right
item.description: I love it when a company challenges itself to make a cheaper version of a beloved product. Xreal's $299 A01 Plus is a stripped-down version of its $449 1S that's light on features but with just...

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over...

item.title: Doom’s new expansion shows just what makes id Software special
item.description: This week should have been a celebration for Doom developer id Software. The studio just launched Revelations, a meaty expansion for Doom: The Dark Ages that adds a powerful new weapon and more...

item.title: Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
item.description: A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch. FoxIO...

item.title: From 17,000 to 1.1 Million Assets: How Lumen Technologies Rebuilt Exposure Management at Scale
item.description: Most enterprises assume their asset inventory is close enough to accurate. The evidence suggests otherwise. According to a survey of over 600 security leaders in the 2026 Axonius Actionability...

item.title: Exposed Hacker Server Reveals WP-SHELLSTORM Backdooring Thousands of WordPress Sites
item.description: A cybercrime crew left one of its own servers wide open on the internet for three weeks, and it exposed the operation's inner workings: the hacking tools, the activity logs, and target lists...

item.title: AI Surveillance and Social Progress
item.description: In the near future, AI-powered surveillance systems will be able to track everything we do in public, and much of what we do in private. And if we do something wrong—shoplift, litter, jaywalk, you...

item.title: Polestar owners left ‘holding the bag’ after EV brand pulls out of the US
item.description: Last month, Polestar shocked the auto industry when it announced that it was pulling out of the US. The EV company's decision came after the federal government denied its authorization to continue...

item.title: Study of 281 Free Android VPN Apps Finds Traffic Leaks, Unencrypted Data, and Tracking
item.description: Researchers ran 281 of the most popular free VPN apps on the Google Play Store through a new testing system and found that many fail at the basics people install a VPN for, i.e., keeping their...

item.title: Instagram and Facebook will likely require a redesign after EU rules they’re ‘addictive’
item.description: Meta is in breach of the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA), a preliminary investigation has found, over the "addictive" design of Instagram and Facebook. It's likely to be forced to redesign both...

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Microsoft Entra Passkey Enrollment to Gain Microsoft 365 Access
item.description: A threat actor has been targeting organizations spanning multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that prompt Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey with an aim to carry...

item.title: A New Experiential Gallery Just Might Change Your Mind About AI Art
item.description: Billed as the “world’s first museum of AI arts,” Dataland uses wearables and troves of material from the Amazon to merge nature, biometrics, and art.

item.title: Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain Over $5 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Security firm Coinspect has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. The flaw is in how some wallet software generated its recovery phrase, the words...
